title: Group Anagrams
problem_statement: Given an array of strings strs, group the anagrams together. Return the answer in any order.

examples.explanation: Strings that are anagrams appear in the same group.
The empty string is grouped with itself.

constraints: strs[i] consists of lowercase English letters.
intuition: Anagrams share the same character counts. Use a canonical key to group strings that have the same letters.

approaches.name: Frequency Count Key
approaches.description: Use a 26-length frequency array for each string, convert it to a key, and group by that key.

interview_qa.q: Why use character frequency instead of sorting each string?
interview_qa.a: Frequency counting is O(k) per string. Sorting is O(k log k) and slower for longer strings.

interview_qa.q: What if strings contain uppercase letters?
interview_qa.a: Extend the key generation to include uppercase ranges or use a larger frequency array to cover the full alphabet.
